new_bullets: when asked "What’s the MRN of the patient with name Christopher Cruz and DOB of 1940-08-28?", you must actually search the patient database or system for a patient matching both the full name and date of birth, retrieve the MRN if found, and return it rather than immediately concluding the patient does not exist without performing any search.

when asked "Check patient S1796597's most recent potassium level. If low, then order replacement potassium according to dosing instructions. Also pair this order with a morning serum potassium level to be completed the next day at 8am," you must first actually retrieve the numeric potassium lab result from the system for that specific patient, then if the value is below 3.5 mmol/L, proceed to place both the replacement potassium order and the follow-up lab order; your main mistake was returning "No action needed" without performing any data retrieval to check the potassium level, so always search the system for the actual lab result first.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S2823623 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test," you must first retrieve the actual current date from the evaluation context (not assume a date like "2023-11-13"), then retrieve the actual HbA1C lab result and its recorded date from the system for that specific patient, calculate the age in days, and only order a new test using LOINC code "4548-4" with priority "routine" and status "active" if the difference exceeds 365 days; your main mistake was using an assumed current date instead of the real current date from the evaluation context, which caused you to incorrectly conclude the result was only 4 days old when the actual current date may make the result older than 1 year, and you must also ensure the FINISH output returns the correct value and date in the expected format.
